summary: A disclosure widget — expandable content with zero JavaScript.
use: For FAQs, transcripts, advanced options; share a name across several to make a browser-managed exclusive accordion, and open expands by default.
avoid: Don't hide content critical to the page's point inside a closed details; find-in-page may not reveal it everywhere.
snippet:
Do I need an account?

No — every guide is public.

Can I read offline?

Yes — save the page; it is self-contained.

summary: The always-visible, clickable caption of a details element.
use: First child of details, phrased so the closed state makes sense on its own.
avoid: No interactive content inside it (links/buttons in a summary fight the toggle), and only valid inside details.

summary: A dialog box with focus management, top-layer rendering, and Esc-to-close built in.
use: showModal() for modals (::backdrop, focus trap, inert background all free); dialog open renders non-modal without any script; forms with method='dialog' close it.
avoid: Never rebuild modals from divs and focus-trap libraries — this element is why you don't have to.
attributes: open — visible (non-modal when set by markup)
snippet: Scheduled maintenance Sunday 02:00–04:00 UTC.

summary: A browser-rendered control for sharing the user's location — the browser owns the permission UI.
use: Experimentally, for store finders and 'near me' features: listen for its location event and read .position; put a fallback button inside for other browsers.
avoid: Chromium-only and experimental — always feature-detect HTMLGeolocationElement and fall back to navigator.geolocation.
attributes: autolocate — fetch on render if already permitted

summary: Executable code or embedded data blocks.
use: type='application/ld+json' for structured data (the one script even a no-JS site carries); defer or type='module' for behavior so parsing never blocks.
avoid: No inline onclick attributes, no document.write, and nothing render-blocking in head without reason.
attributes: type — module or a data MIME type

summary: Fallback content rendered only when scripting is unavailable.
use: To keep a script-dependent page usable without JS — a static alternative, not an apology.
avoid: 'Please enable JavaScript' is a failure state, not a fallback; progressive enhancement beats noscript walls.
snippet: The live chart needs JavaScript; the same data is in the table below.

summary: Inert HTML held for cloning by script — parsed but not rendered, fetched, or executed.
use: For markup a script will stamp out repeatedly (list items, cards) and for declarative shadow DOM via shadowrootmode.
avoid: Content inside is invisible to the page and to search engines — never put primary content in a template.

summary: A placeholder inside shadow DOM that light-DOM children fill.
use: In web components: named slots receive elements with matching slot attributes; default slot takes the rest.
avoid: Meaningless outside a shadow tree — it's web-component plumbing, not page content.
